title: Vietnam War escalation
description: United States combat troops arrived in South Vietnam as the war expanded into a deeper military commitment.
historical_context: Vietnam War escalation / Cold War policy / public unease

title: Tet Offensive
description: A coordinated offensive across South Vietnam shocked the public and changed the emotional meaning of the war.
historical_context: Vietnam War / media shock / anti-war movement

title: Woodstock
description: A counterculture music festival became a symbol of youth resistance, communal hope, and anti-war feeling.
historical_context: Anti-war movement / 1960s counterculture / folk and rock culture

title: Kent State shootings
description: National Guard troops killed four students during protests against the expansion of the Vietnam War.
historical_context: Anti-war protests / state violence / public grief

title: Paris Peace Accords
description: An agreement intended to establish peace in Vietnam and end direct United States involvement in the war.
historical_context: Vietnam War / peace negotiations / uncertain transition

title: Pat Garrett & Billy the Kid
description: The film and its music carried a weary farewell atmosphere in the same cultural moment as the war's attempted closure.
historical_context: 1970s film culture / Bob Dylan era / farewell and mortality

title: Fall of Saigon
description: The capture of Saigon marked the end of the Vietnam War and left images of departure, loss, and unresolved memory.
